channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iranian President Warns US of Consequences Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: As tensions escalate between Iran and the United States, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has issued a rare open letter to the American public condemning what he calls acts of aggression by the Tru...

item.title: Mandeni Workers Urge Health Department to Improve Overcrowded Sinikithemba Clinic
item.description: Retail and informal workers at Mandeni Shopping Centre in northern KwaZulu-Natal are urging the government to upgrade the Sinikithemba Clinic, which they say is too small, poorly equipped and unable t...

item.title: Rungwe landslide kills multiple children; grandfather dies by suicide from grief
item.description: Mbeya region in Tanzania has suffered severe loss following heavy rains, especially in Rungwe District. On the night of 25 March 2026, heavy rain caused landslides that claimed the lives of more than ...

item.title: Uhuru Urges Politicians to Focus on Unity and Transformative Leadership
item.description: During the funeral of former Kirinyaga senator Daniel Karaba, former President Uhuru Kenyatta called on Kenyan leaders to move away from tribalism and insults, urging them to embrace integrity, unity,...

item.title: Open Society Raises Concerns Over Brian Kagoro's Denied Entry at JKIA
item.description: Open Society Foundations has raised serious concerns after their Managing Director of Programmes, Brian Kagoro, was detained and denied entry into Kenya at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA) w...

item.title: Residents hit hard as paraffin prices surge across Gauteng
item.description: The price of paraffin has sharply increased in parts of Gauteng, with some communities now paying as much as R28 per litre following a major price adjustment by the Department of Minerals and Petroleu...

item.title: EJ Obiena: Elevating Filipino Excellence in Pole Vaulting
item.description: EJ Obiena, the Philippines’ top pole vaulter, continues to push the boundaries of Filipino athletic excellence. Known for breaking the 6-meter barrier, Obiena has not only achieved world-class status ...

item.title: Bureau of Customs takes eight of Zaldy Co’s luxury cars amid investigation
item.description: The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has taken custody of eight luxury vehicles out of the 18 previously seized that are linked to former Ako Bicol Representative Zaldy Co. These vehicles were taken from the I...

item.title: Gauteng Premier Appoints EFF’s Dunga as Finance MEC in Cabinet Reshuffle
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has announced a significant reshuffle in his executive council, appointing Nkululeko Dunga from the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) as the province's new finance MEC. Th...

item.title: Federal trial for Luigi Mangione rescheduled to October amid state case timing
item.description: A federal trial for Luigi Mangione, accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, has been rescheduled from September to October rather than being postponed until next year as requested by h...

item.title: Moss & Fog Introduces Artisanal Fog and Premium Moss for Sale
item.description: Moss & Fog, a lifestyle brand known for promoting slow, thoughtful, and aesthetically pleasing experiences, has announced a unique product line: artisanal fog and premium moss. Their new offerings inc...

item.title: Tanasha Donna Defends Her Son Against Online Criticism
item.description: Kenyan singer Tanasha Donna, who is also known for her relationship with Tanzanian star Diamond Platnumz, has openly addressed critics targeting her young son, Naseeb Junior. Recently, Tanasha shared ...

item.title: South Africans Protest Over Installation of Igbo Cultural Leader in Eastern Cape
item.description: Civil society organisations and members of the Progressive Forces of South Africa staged a demonstration outside the Nigerian High Commission in Pretoria over the recent installation of an Igbo cultur...

item.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Facelift Recovery and What to Expect
item.description: Recovering from a facelift is a gradual process that extends well beyond the operating theatre. The surgery, known as a full facelift or rhytidectomy, targets sagging skin, jowls, and underlying facia...

item.title: Brooke Bellamy addresses RecipeTin Eats plagiarism claims one year later
item.description: Brooke Bellamy, owner of Brooki Bakehouse, has publicly addressed the plagiarism controversy involving popular cooking blog RecipeTin Eats, a year after the issue arose. The dispute drew widespread at...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over ER Whistleblower Safety, Lobbying Transparency, and Firearms Buyback Shortfalls

item.title: UK Prime Minister Seeks Stronger European Partnership Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the United Kingdom has said that the ongoing war in Iran is causing global instability and making it important for Britain to focus on strengthening economic and defence...

item.title: Rising Fuel Prices Push Up Costs for South African Consumers and Businesses
item.description: South Africans are facing new financial pressures after a sharp increase in fuel prices that took effect on 1 April 2026. Diesel jumped by R7.51 per litre and petrol by R3.06 following disruptions in ...

item.title: Solriamfetol improves alertness in early-morning shift workers with shift work disorder
item.description: A recent clinical trial led by researchers at Mass General Brigham has shown that the wakefulness-promoting drug solriamfetol significantly improves alertness and daily functioning in early-morning sh...
